observations.recommendation:
2.6	We have recommended, and management has agreed, to direct the Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines in COA Circular No. 2023-008 for the proper write-off of irrecoverable receivables, subject to approval by appropriate authorities.
2.7	Moving forward, we have recommended that management enhance internal control mechanisms to prevent similar occurrences by enforcing periodic reviews, reconciliations, and validation of receivable accounts.

We have recommended, and management has agreed, to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure full disclosure of the hold-out deposit in the Notes to Financial Statements, specifying that it is restricted and serves as collateral for the DBP loan, to ensure accurate financial reporting and compliance with Section 27 of IPSAS 1 and Section 59 of IPSAS 2; and
Ensure that all information subject to disclosure under the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) is accurately and comprehensively presented in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Immediately transfer the unexpended balance of the 70% Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF)-MOOE, totaling ₱831,938.08, from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-02;
Ensure that all unutilized LDRRMF balances are properly monitored and transferred to the STF at the end of each fiscal year to prevent similar instances in the future; and
Ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-02 and other relevant laws and regulations to guarantee effective management of the LDRRMF.

We have recommended, and management has agreed, to:
Ensure strict compliance with PhilHealth regulations to avoid delays in remittances and the imposition of penalties and interest, which are disallowable in audit;
Direct the concerned personnel to exercise due diligence in uploading payroll contribution amounts into the Electronic Premium Remittance System (EPRS), ensuring consistency between actual payroll deductions and the system-generated Statement of Account (SOA); and
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to reflect the accurate balance of the Due to PhilHealth account as of December 31, 2024.

We have recommended, and management has agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the accounting for cash advances: debit the Advances to Officers and Employees account upon granting a cash advance, and debit the appropriate traveling expenses account only upon liquidating the cash advance.

We have recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Mayor to expedite the reconstitution and operationalization of the Local Project Monitoring Committee (LPMC), including the issuance of a formal designation of the Chairperson and Secretariat;
Direct all concerned departments and offices to prepare and submit to the Audit Team, through the LPMC, a comprehensive list of all infrastructure and non-infrastructure programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) to be implemented within the year, as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular 2013-004;
Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), in coordination with the LPMC, to submit Quarterly Reports on Government PPAs not later than the 10th day after the end of each quarter; and
Designate a responsible officer to inform the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader within ten (10) days after the award of any infrastructure project or before the start of project implementation, confirming that the appropriate signboards and notices have been posted, per Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2013-004.
The management commented that it is in the process of reconstituting and reorganizing the Local Project Monitoring Committee (LPMC) in coordination with the DILG. The Municipal Planning and Development Office is being considered as the Secretariat. Once reconstituted, the LPMC will handle the monitoring and evaluation of both nationally and locally funded programs and projects. This initiative aims to address audit findings related to the non-submission of PPAs and quarterly reports by institutionalizing standard procedures and ensuring timely and accurate reporting, thereby promoting transparency, accountability, and good governance.
The audit team acknowledged management’s initiative to reconstitute the LPMC and designate the MPDO as its Secretariat. However, the delayed and incomplete submission of the required list of PPAs and quarterly reports for CY 2024 hindered audit validation and remains a non-compliance with COA Circular 2013-004.
We reiterate the need for the immediate operationalization of the LPMC, submission of complete and timely reports covering all programs and projects, and designation of a responsible officer to ensure compliance with reporting and notification requirements. These measures are essential to uphold transparency and accountability in local governance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to enforce monitoring of collection remittance by requiring the collecting officers to remit daily and intact the full amount so received and collected. However, when collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, he shall require remittance/deposit at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00 pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD 1445 and Item 32, Chapter II of Revised Cash Examination Manual.

Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an Inventory of all municipal road networks and provide the GSO and the Accountant a copy thereof.

Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for each road network and to report the same in the proper format using the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks where the different road components are detailed and road network ID number is indicated.

Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for each local road network and to identify the unrecorded Road Networks.

Moreover, we advise the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness of the documents before affixing his/her signature to the Disbursement Vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Human Resource Officer to furnish the Accounting Office with a copy of the schedule of earned leave credits of LGU personnel and to direct the OIC Accountant to accrue the money value of earned leave to be recorded as liability or disclose it in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
2.8	We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor to authorize the General Services Officer (GSO) to inquire with the Registry of Deeds on whether the above-mentioned lots were titled.
After inquiry, the Municipality may take the following actions to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 2 of PD No. 1445:
a.	If the said lots were not titled, direct the General Service Officer (GSO) to expedite the processing of the Certificate of Land Title.
b.	If the said lots were titled but were not registered in the Municipality's name, require the GSO to secure the Transfer Certificate of Title in the Municipality’s name.

3.11	We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all of the Municipality’s depository accounts to establish the monthly adjusted bank balance, and submit them to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of the month; prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, staled checks and unrecorded deposits to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance and be authorized to enroll the municipality’s bank accounts in “Landbank weAccess” so that bank statements can be readily available at any time.

4.11	We recommended to the management the following:
a.	Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Certificate of List of all Real Property Tax Collectibles to the Accounting Office at the beginning of the year; and
b.	Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the RPT/SET Receivable accounts based on the certified list using the modified accrual method to establish a realistic collection target on Real Property Tax. Also, the corresponding subsidiary ledger shall be maintained to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

5.11	We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant reclassify the current and non-current portion of its outstanding loans payable account to present fairly in the financial statements and furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Amortization Schedule for Equal Principal.

6.8	We recommended that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
a.	Enforce monitoring of collection and remittance by requiring the collecting officers to remit daily and intact the full amount so received and collected. However, when collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, he shall require remittance/deposit at least once a week, or as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00 pursuant to Section 69(1) of PD 1445 and Item 32, Chapter II of Revised Cash Examination Manual;
b.	Designate a liquidating officer from among the collectors. The liquidating officer shall perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections turned over to him, after which turn over intact the cash collections to the Municipal Treasurer; and
c.	Instruct all accountable officers to update the cashbook daily or upon the occurrence of transaction, and reconcile the balance with the cash on hand and monitor the compliance thereof.

7.10	We have recommended to the management the following:
a.	To include Project Inspection Report and Statement of Time Elapsed to its punch-           list of the basic essential required documents to support the claims for progress and final payments to the contractors to ensure the quality of the cumulative value of work and in accordance with the approved plans and specifications;
b.	To strictly observe the proper and complete documentation of any claims against government funds by strengthening internal control through submission of the above-mentioned documents before disbursing government funds; and
c.	To assign additional staff in the Office of the Municipal Engineer who will help with the preparation of the required paperwork in order to improve the said Office's operational efficiency.

8.8	We have recommended to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to ensure adequate planning of projects, programs or activities to be funded from LDRRMF and optimally utilize the fund to enable the community to fully benefit from projects, programs, or activities that are charged to it.
The LDRRMO should apprise LDRRMC on the issues and concerns and most particularly the financial and physical status of the implementation of the PPAs under LDRRMF.

9.11	We have recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Treasurer
Ensure that all collecting officers are covered with sufficient fidelity bond before allowing them to discharge their duties and functions; and
Together with the Accountable Officers, monitor the effectivity and expiration date of their fidelity bonds and see to it that renewal thereof be made before their expiration.
Stop allowing the AOs to perform their duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that collections/remittances of AOs do not exceed the maximum accountability covered by their fidelity bonds in order to protect the interest of the Municipal Government.

10.6	We have recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to closely oversee the handling of accountable forms once they are issued to accountable officers. In addition, the transfer of accountable forms from one accountable officer to another should be discontinued.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the proper disposition of the five accounts. If there are no other outstanding obligations chargeable to the funds, the municipality shall take the following actions:

For funds accruing to NGAs, return immediately the unexpended balance to the source agency;

For counterpart funds, the Local Finance Committee to conduct the necessary evaluation and thereafter give recommendations on the proper disposition of the funds in relation to the priority projects of the municipality; and

Initiate the closure of the bank accounts once the balance is returned to the source agency and/or transferred to the general fund (counterpart funds).
observations.managementAction:
Reviewed the five bank accounts.

Returned unexpended balance

Evaluated the accounts

Prepared necessary documents for the closure of bank accounts.
observations.reason:
The counterpart funds remained in the trust fund and were not yet transferred to the General Fund.

The documents needed to close the bank accounts are currently being prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all of its depository accounts in order to establish the monthly adjusted bank balance, and submit them to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of the month.; and
As signatory of the bank accounts, authorize the OIC-Municipal Accountant to enroll their accounts in Landbank weAccess so that bank statements could be readily available at any time.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
To ensure conduct of complete physical inventory taking, create an Inventory Committee to be headed by the General Services Officer, and include in the committee the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, all concerned department heads, or any representative of the said department heads. The inventory taking shall be witnessed by any representative of the Commission on Audit.
Direct the General Services Officer to maintain the Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and incorporate the balances in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to update the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and reconcile the balance against the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Direct the General Service Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the PPEs that has no documents or records by using the Fair Market Value.
Direct the General Service Officer to ensure all semi-expendable equipment and properties issued for official use of officers and employees of the municipality are covered by Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) and Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR), respectively. Also, the GSO shall ensure that all equipment/property are assigned with Inventory Tag/Stickers. The property sticker shall contain all vital information of each property/equipment for easy identification such as: brief description of the property/equipment; model number; serial number; property number; acquisition date; and to whom assigned/responsible Officer/Employee.
Direct the General Services Officer to identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
Direct the concerned personnel to conduct one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in order to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, which prescribes the guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, recognition of those found at station and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the municipality.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee as well as the Technical Working Group to ensure that all procurements of Infrastructure are included in the Annual Procurement Plan and all implementation thereof are conducted thru public bidding. In the event that implementation by administration is more practicable and advantageous, it shall ensure full compliance with the conditions provided under GPPB Resolution No. 20-2020; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to accomplish and submit the Approved Program of Works, Detailed Cost Estimate, Status of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, pictures and Certificates of Completion for every infrastructure project implemented by administration.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the LDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the list of projects/program/activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and to submit a revised Investment Plan to be approved by the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive.
Require the LDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team. The report shall be prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Designate special disbursing officers for special purpose/time-bound undertaking and ensure that they are covered by sufficient fidelity bond in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular No. 2006-005 dated July 13, 2006;
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the fidelity bond of designated special disbursing officers; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the accounting for cash advances: debit the Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account upon grant of cash advance and debit the appropriate expense account only upon liquidation of the cash advance.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Real Property Tax Account Register/Certified List of Taxpayers to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
Based on the list, require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to take up in the books the RPT/SET Receivable accounts using the modified accrual method in order to establish a realistic collection target on Real Property Tax. Also, the corresponding subsidiary ledger shall be maintained to monitor tax collections and delinquencies.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the GSO to take control and custody of the supplies held for consumption/distribution, strictly follow the procedures in the requisition and issuance of inventory items, and maintain stock cards;
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchase of drugs and medicines and other supplies and materials as inventory, recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances, and maintain stock ledger cards; and
Provide storage/stockroom with adequate space to accommodate and safeguard all the supplies and materials before consumption, issuance or distribution.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the reconciliation of their records in order to submit the monthly report of collections and deposits together with the official receipts to the Office of the Auditor within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month. Thus, facilitating timely audit of collections.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the General Services Officer to submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of construction and heavy equipment; strictly comply with the provisions of the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 to be properly guided in the documentation of fuel consumption and the use of government vehicles/equipment as well as to be able to regulate fuel consumption.
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that payments for fuel expenses are duly supported with duly filled up driver’s trip tickets, charge slips, and billing statements for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption before recording the same in the books of accounts.
Require the heavy equipment operators to present a duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets before use of the said equipment.
Direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure Approved Program of Works and Detailed Cost Estimates are readily available to support every request of fuel and to ensure that actual fuel consumption of construction and heavy equipment does not exceed the approved quantity of fuel estimated to be used in the project.
Require the gas station to issue charge slips every request of fuel and to send a monthly billing statement to support every claim for fuel/gasoline.
Submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption not later than ten (10) days of the succeeding month for validation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to use the appropriate accounts in the recording of transactions in the books of accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, and to submit the list of recipients/beneficiaries of the rice and other dry goods.

We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the HRMO and other concerned personnel to exert more effort in giving priority to appoint a licensed Certified Public Accountant as Municipal Accountant.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to harness or develop her competency or efficiency by attending seminars and trainings.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain subsidiary ledgers of all accounts noted in Appendix A to comply with the provisions of Sections 111(1) of PD 1445 and Section 361 of the Government Accounting Manual on LGU.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a.	Together with other concerned personnel, assess the situations/problems/lapses regarding the delayed submission of reports and documents and adopt an effective and efficient method in order to fasten the submission thereof, and
b.	Submit financial reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular no. 2009-006.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit financial reports within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular no. 2009-006, and Section 70, Chapter 4 of New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for LGUs.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Heads, the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), and the other personnel involved in the preparation of the Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders to ensure that the same are adequately accomplished as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2002 – 003 and COA Circular No 96-010, and to attend seminars on the preparation of procurement documents. Moreover, we advise the Municipal Accountant to verify the completeness of the documents before affixing his/her signature to the Disbursement Vouchers.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned personnel to stamp “PAID” all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents upon payment and indicate the date and number of the check issued. These procedures lessen the chance of paying the same claims more than once. Thus, safeguarding cash from loss thru double payments.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the timeline of the submission of approved contracts and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. All approved contracts and their supporting documents should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of contract to facilitate timely review and evaluation of contracts;
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to attach to the disbursement vouchers the following documents as supporting documents to payment of progress and/or final billings:
Contractor's letter of request for payment of (1st to final) billing,
Statement of work accomplishment (SWA) as of the period
Contractor's letter of request for inspection
Inspection report by LGU engineering office
Photo Documentation of construction in progress
Photocopy of paid vouchers 1st to final billing
Require the BAC Secretariat to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents enumerated in Annex A.

We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:
a.	Hire a Certified Public Accountant to manage the municipality’s financial transactions.
b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to harness or develop her competency or efficiency by attending seminars and trainings.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: For CY 2021, the OIC-Municipal Accountant failed to prepare and submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a.	Create an Inventory Committee to be headed by the General Services Officer and include in the committee the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant as well as all department heads.

b.	Direct the General Service Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the PPEs that has no documents or records by using the Fair Market Value

c.	Direct the General Service Officer maintain the Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and incorporate the balances in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: Conducted partial inventory of PPE
observations.reason:
The management had encountered difficulties in completing the inventory of PPE.

For CY 2021, there was no Report of Physical Count of PPE submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. 	Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an Inventory of all municipal road networks and provide the GSO and the Accountant a copy thereof.

b. 	Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for each road network and to report the same in the proper format using the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks where the different road components are detailed and road network ID number is indicated.

c. 	Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for each local road network and to identify the unrecorded Road Networks.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Inventory of all municipal road networks was not conducted by concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the following adjusting entries:

We have recommended to the local chief executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the LGU personnel to submit transportation tickets or a certification that a private vehicle was used during the official travels.
observations.managementAction: Submitted required documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the local chief executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the municipal treasurer to submit an acknowledgement receipt duly signed by the recipients or their authorized representative.
observations.managementAction: Submitted required documents.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the BAC Secretariat and the Municipal Engineer to designate personnel to monitor and ensure the timely submission of completed contracts, and to submit all perfected and awarded contracts together with all the documents forming part thereof, as well as documents required in Annexes of said circular in order to facilitate timely auditorial and legal review as well as technical review and inspection.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Municipal Vice-Mayor to apply for fidelity bond and approve and countersign the disbursement vouchers and checks for the appropriation of the Sanggunian.
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to fast track the appointment of the vacant positions in the plantilla to ensure that vital public services are delivered to the public.
observations.reason: Mandatory positions such as Municipal Health Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor remained vacant.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer, and Bids and Awards Secretariat to submit the above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: Some of the financial reports are submitted within the prescribed timeline.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate and the LDRRMO, to prepare and submit a report on the sources and utilization of the MDRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Prepared monthly reports.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee as well as the Technical Working Group to ensure that all procurement of Infrastructure are included in the Annual Procurement Plan and all implementation thereof are conducted thru public bidding. In event that implementation by administration is more practicable and advantageous, ensure that the conditions are complied with.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of projects by administration are still being practiced but limited only to small projects whose amount does not exceed 1,000,000.00

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Vice-Mayor to apply for fidelity bond and approve and countersign the disbursement vouchers and checks for the appropriation of the Sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: LCE approves the DVs ad Checks charged against the budget for the Legislative Department

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate and the LDRRMO, to prepare and submit a report on the sources and utilization of the MDRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Reports were not submitted monthly to the COA

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to insure the LGUs properties with the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in order to protect its properties and mitigate the risk of loss in case of calamity and disasters.
observations.managementAction: LGU have not availed of Insurance

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the LDRRM Council to include the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the Annual Investment Plan of the LDRRM Fund in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: LGU has no approves AIP for the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:
Hire a Certified Public Accountant to manage the municipality’s financial transactions.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to harness or develop her competency or efficiency by attending seminars and trainings.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create an Inventory Committee to be headed by the General Services Officer and include in the committee the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant as well as all department heads.
Direct the General Service Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to record the PPEs that has no documents or records by using the Fair Market Value.
Direct the General Services Officer maintain the Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and incorporate the balances in the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).
Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant to update the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and reconcile the balance against the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an inventory of all municipal road networks and provide the GSO and the Accountant a copy thereof.
Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for each road network and to report the same in the proper format using the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks where the different road components are detailed and road network ID number is indicated.
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for each local road network and to identify the unrecorded Road Networks.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the following adjusting entries:

Account | Debit | Credit
Grants and Donations in Cash | P12,897,971 | 
Subsidy from National Government |  | P12,897,971

observations.managementAction: GSO have commenced physical inventory on PPEs and is currently  securing data for the creation of property cards in coordination with the Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

a.	Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for each road network and to report the same in the proper format using the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks where the different road components are detailed and road network ID number is indicated.

b.	Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for each local road network and to identify the unrecorded Road Network amounting to P10,242,277.

c.	Direct the GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of road network accounts.
observations.managementAction: GSO and Accountant is currently  securing data for the creation of property cards.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant Designate to:

a. 	Record the completed Projects in the appropriate Property Cards; and 
b. 	Record the following adjusting entries in the Journal Entry Voucher:
observations.managementAction: Accountant has recorded and made necessary adjustments to recognize the unrecorded depreciation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:

a. 	Hire a Certified Public Accountant to manage the municipality’s financial transactions.

b. 	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines, and grant authority to the Municipal Accountant Designate to access the on line account in order to facilitate the immediate viewing of balances for printing.

c. 	Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2018, and to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2019 within the prescribed period, together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: LGU have enrolled in the we-access of the LBP in order to comply with the prompt submission of BRS.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the collectors through the Municipal Treasurer to maintain cashbook, reconcile the balances obtained at the end of the day with the cash on hand, and issue certification at the end of every month.
observations.managementAction: Collector are now maintaining cashbooks.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to use a separate Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) for the Special Education Tax collections and to stop the recording of these collections in the Cash book for General Fund considering that it is a separate fund.
observations.managementAction: Separate RCD for SEF are being maintained.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer to submit all perfected and awarded contracts together with all the documents forming part thereof, as well as documents required in Annexes of said circular, to the audit team in order to facilitate timely auditorial and legal review as well as technical review and inspection by the Technical Audit Sector, COA, Regional Office No X.
observations.managementAction: Most Contracts for 2019 were submitted, however CY 2020 Contracts were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Human Resource Officer to furnish the Accounting Office with a copy of the schedule of earned leave credits of LGU personnel and to direct the OIC Accountant to accrue the money value of earned leave to be recorded as liability or disclose it in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Allocation of budget for earned leave credits are made only for those retiring officers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request in compliance with the instruction in filling up the Obligation Request as provided in COA Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.
observations.managementAction: Details are provided for in the Budget Forms.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer, and Bids and Awards Secretariat to submit the above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: Some of the financial reports are submitted within the prescribed timeline.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant Designate to prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance in order to aid the management and the auditor in monitoring the financial position of the agency.
observations.managementAction: Trial Balances were submitted

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:

Hire a Certified Public Accountant to manage the municipality’s financial transactions.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line E-ACCESS in the Land Bank of the Philippines, and grant access to the Municipal Accountant Designate in order to facilitate the immediate viewing of balances.

Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2018, and to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2019 within the prescribed period, together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The designated accountant does not have the required skills coupled with the large volume of transactions and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate and the LDRRMO, to prepare and submit a report on the sources and utilization of the MDRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: No Action Taken, LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant Designate failed to submit Monthly Utilization Report to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Municipal Vice-Mayor to apply for fidelity bond and approve and countersign the disbursement vouchers and checks for the appropriation of the Sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Vice Mayor failed to apply for Bond.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer, and Bids and Awards Secretariat to submit the above mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Vouchers were submitted by the Municipal Accountant Designate beyond the required period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the LDRRM Council to include the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the Annual Investment Plan of the LDRRM Fund in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: No AIP were prepared and approved for the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct both the General Services Officer and the OIC Municipal Accountant to maintain Stock Cards for inventories and the Property Cards for PPEs and subsidiary ledger cards for each property, plant and equipment respectively;
Require the General Services Officer to identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal;
Direct the OIC Municipal Accountant:
To update the Supplies Ledger Card (SLC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC); and
Prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of the inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
The concerned Local Officials committed to make, maintain, update, and reconcile their records.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC) for each road network and to report the same in the proper format using the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Networks where the different road components are detailed and road network ID number is indicated.
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to create and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) for each local road network and to identify the unrecorded Road Network amounting to P10,242,277.
Direct the GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of road network accounts.
The concerned Local Officials committed to make, maintain, update, and reconcile their records.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant Designate to:
Record the completed Projects in the appropriate Property Cards; and
Record the following adjusting entries in the Journal Entry Voucher:
(Please see Annex A for the detailed computation of depreciation)
The concerned local officials committed to make and maintain the required records and take up the necessary adjustments.

observations.managementAction: The GSO was able to conduct the Annual Physical Inventory for CY 2018 and CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to review all completed projects and record them in the appropriate property plant and equipment accounts in the General Fund for completed projects, and appropriate Construction In Progress accounts in the Trust Fund for the on-going projects, using the following entries as adjustments entries:
observations.managementAction: The unrecorded PPE were duly recorded in the CY 2019 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer to submit all perfected and awarded contracts together with all the documents forming part thereof, as well as documents required in Annexes of said circular, to the audit team in order to facilitate timely auditorial and legal review as well as technical review and inspection by the COA.
observations.managementAction: BAC was able to submit some of the perfected contracts but beyond the required period. Also, there were perfected contracts for CY 2019 that were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive: 

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to institute a system such as E-Tracs that could facilitate the prompt determination, assessment, and collection of real property tax;

Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to record Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year; and

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection of Real Property Taxes and to give emphasis on the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by applying the administrative remedies against delinquent taxpayers as provided for under the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: Collectible Real Property and Special Education Tax were taken up in the book of accounts.
Solid Waste Management Plan was duly approved, and Ordinance for Solid Waste Management was duly enacted an implemented.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request in compliance with the instruction in filling up the Obligation Request as provided in COA Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer were able to fill-up the some of the necessary details in the Obligation Request but there were still missing information.
Report for Accountability for Accountable Forms were duly prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a municipal accountant and, if funds are available and enough, to hire additional employees to be assigned in the office of the municipal accountant. Otherwise, require the designated municipal accountant to render overtime to prepare and submit monthly trial balances on time for proper monitoring and timely decision making of the management.
observations.managementAction: Semestral Trial balance were prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to Local Chief Executive to direct the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports, respectively to this Office.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person were able to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: Apply for the fidelity bond, approve the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the Sanggunian and be allowed to countersign the checks drawn.
observations.managementAction: No Action Taken
observations.reason: The Vice-Mayor was not able to approve the disbursement vouchers appropriated for the operation of the Sanggunian because he was not able to secure a bond

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to complete the details that should be provided in the Obligation Request in compliance with COA Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006.
observations.managementAction: Obligation Request has no FPP and account code indicated.
observations.reason: Budget Officer was newly appointed and was not well familiar with the FPP and Budget code.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal General Services Officer, and Bids and Awards Secretariat to submit the required reports within the prescribed timetable for timely post-audit and verification.
observations.managementAction: Delayed/non submission of reports.
observations.reason: Lack of CPA and manpower

observations.recommendation: Include the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the Annual Investment Plan of the LDRRM Fund in order to utilize the same to fund LDRRM projects/programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: No AIP for the previous years unexpended LDRRM Fund because budget for CY 2018 were already approved.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:
Hire a Certified Public Accountant to manage the municipality’s financial transactions.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line E-ACCESS in the Land Bank of the Philippines, and grant access to the Municipal Accountant Designate in order to facilitate the immediate viewing of balances.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2018, and to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2019 within the prescribed period, together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting documents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to review all completed projects and record them in the appropriate property plant and equipment accounts in the General Fund for completed projects, and appropriate Construction In Progress accounts in the Trust Fund for the on-going projects, using the following entries as adjustments entries:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines/Development Bank of the Philippines/Philippine National Bank and grant access to the Municipal Accountant in order that she can view immediately the balances on line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.

Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements on time together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting Documents if there are any, otherwise, it should be based on the printed on-line balances taken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant Designate was able to prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation for CY 2017. The Municipal Treasurer was not able to enroll in e-access because they are contemplating of transferring their bank accounts in Land Bank – Kapatagan.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive to create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment to be headed by the General Services Officer.

The Municipal Accountant Designate to make the necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant Designate to prepare and maintain a stock card and subsidiary ledger card respectively on each type of equipment after the physical count and report of the PPE’s are completed.

The Municipal Accountant Designate to provide residual value in computing the depreciation and the fully depreciated property account should be separated on the corresponding property account.

The Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS to be covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost, damaged properties without covering insurance, the liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer was able to prepare and submit the Inventory Report for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Prepare and submit a report on the sources and utilization of the MDRRMF to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, for verification/audit purposes.
observations.managementAction: Report of Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund was submitted but on quarterly basis

observations.recommendation: Establish the position for the three (3) staff responsible for the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning in the regular Plantilla item, in accordance with NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No.2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.managementAction: The municipality was able to establish staff for LDRRM Office in the regular Plantilla

observations.recommendation: Request the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an Ordinance to Implement and Support the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000; Intensify campaigns which should be collaborated with the barangay officials concerned; Enforce a continued and stricter implementation of waste segregation, and thorough collection of waste based on set schedules; and Provide budget for the purchase of sanitary landfill.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started implementing provision of RA 9003. However, there is still no Approved Solid Waste Management Plan.

observations.recommendation: Adhere strictly with the rules and regulation in the handling of Accountable Forms; Ensure the effective performance of their functions; Submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms; and Turnover the used and issued Accountable Forms to the Custodian of Accountable Forms.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to submit the Consolidated Monthly Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: Hire a municipal accountant and, if funds permit, additional staff for the municipal accountant's office; or require the designated municipal accountant to render overtime to prepare and submit monthly trial balances on time.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant was able to submit the trail balance but only for 3rd quarter.

observations.recommendation: Direct the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and ensure activities identified as GAD conform with PCW, NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01, and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports to COA.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person was able to submit the GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines/Development Bank of the Philippines/Philippine National Bank and grant access to the Municipal Accountant in order that she can view immediately the balances on line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.
Direct the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements on time together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting Documents if there are any, otherwise, it should be based on the printed on-line balances taken.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive to create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment to be headed by the General Services Officer.
The Municipal Accountant Designate to make the necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant Designate to prepare and maintain a stock card and subsidiary ledger card respectively on each type of equipment after the physical count and report of the PPE’s are completed.
The Municipal Accountant Designate to provide residual value in computing the depreciation and the fully depreciated property account should be separated on the corresponding property account.
The Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS to be covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost, damaged properties without covering insurance, the liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.

We have recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines/Development Bank of the Philippines/Philippine National Bank and grant access to the Municipal Accountant in order that she can view immediately the balances on line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements on time together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting Documents if there are any, otherwise, it should be based on the printed on-line balances taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should require all Revenue Collectors to (1) adhere strictly with the rules and regulation in the handling of Accountable Forms; (2) ensure the effective performance of their functions; (3) submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms, and; (4) turnover the used and issued Accountable Forms.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR | Not Implemented
observations.reason: CRAAF not regularly submitted by the Designated Personnel

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the designated Municipal Accountant to observe existing rules and regulations on the submission of financial reports and disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents to facilitate timely audit of transactions.

We have also recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a Municipal Accountant and, if funds are available and sufficient, to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the municipal accountant. Otherwise, require the designated accountant to render overtime to ensure timely submission of the required reports and documents.
observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR | Not Implemented
observations.reason: DVs are still not submitted within the required period

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a municipal accountant and, if funds are available and sufficient, to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the municipal accountant. Otherwise, require the designated accountant to render overtime to prepare and submit monthly trial balances on time for proper monitoring and timely decision making of the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation to cover the fidelity bond of property officers.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports, respectively to this Office.

observations.managementAction: 2016 AAR | Implemented
observations.reason: SWMP was duly submitted but still pending approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and concerned Municipality Officials adhere and comply with the Department Order No. 27-05 of the DOF and that the PNB accounts deposits be closed and transferred to any AGDB either in the LBP or DBP at the municipality’s discretion, putting however into consideration the security, safety and convenience of all concerned
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR | Implemented.
observations.reason: The municipality is now using its account in Land Bank. They have started with the process of closing their remaining deposit with the Philippine National Bank

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to do away with the practice of drawing cash advance by the municipal treasurer and handing out envelopes to each employee as payments to service rendered during payday in favor of a comprehensive electronic payroll system to pay the employees to lower the risk of fund misuse and result in greater convenience, transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: 2015 AAR | Not Implemented.
observations.reason:
. | There are many employees involve in opening of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to do away with the practice of drawing cash advance by the municipal treasurer and handing out envelopes to each employee as payments to service rendered during payday in favor of a comprehensive electronic payroll system to pay the employees to lower the risk of fund misuse and result in greater convenience, transparency and accountability.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Treasurer to enroll the bank accounts to on-line e-access in the Land Bank of the Philippines/Development Bank of the Philippines/Philippine National Bank and grant access to the Municipal Accountant in order that she can view immediately the balances on line and can possibly print the balances of accounts of the LGU.
The Municipal Accountant to submit the Bank Reconcilitaion Statements on time together with the copies of Bank Statements and other supporting Documents if there are any, otherwise, it should be based on the printed on-line balances taken.

We have recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive to create an appraisal/inventory committee to conduct physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment to be headed by the General Services Officer.
The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of the inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers.
The General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain a stock card and subsidiary ledger card respectively on each type of equipment after the physical count and report of the PPE’s are completed.
The Municipal Accountant to provide residual value in computing the depreciation and the fully depreciated property account should be separated on the corresponding property account.
The Administrative Officer to update the list of properties that needs to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS to be covered by insurance in order to protect the interest of the government. For lost,damaged properties without covering insurance, the liability therefore should devolve upon the parties responsible.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should require all Revenue Collectors to (1) adhere strictly with the rules and regulation in the handling of Accountable Forms; (2) ensure the effective performance of their functions; (3) submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms, and; (4) turnover the used and issued Accountable Forms.

We have recommended to the designated Municipal Accountant to observe existing rules and regulations on the submission of financial reports and disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents to facilitate timely audit of transactions.
We have also recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a Municipal Accountant and, if funds are available and sufficient, to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the municipal accountant. Otherwise, require the designated accountant to render overtime to ensure timely submission of the required reports and documents.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a municipal accountant and, if funds are available and sufficient, to hire additional employee assigned in the office of the municipal accountant. Otherwise, require the designated accountant to render overtime to prepare and submit monthly trial balances on time for proper monitoring and timely decision making of the management.
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation to cover the fidelity bond of property officers.

We have recommended to the GAD Focal person to attend seminar on GAD mainstreaming and to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres and to submit the GAD plan and accomplishment reports, respectively to this Office.
We recommended that the management make a representation to higher authorities for more funds in order to bring more projects to the constituents of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and concerned Municipality Officials adhere and comply with the Department Order No. 27-05 of the DOF and that the PNB accounts deposits be closed and transferred to any AGDB either in the LBP or DBP at the municipality’s discretion, putting however into consideration the security, safety and convenience of all concerned.
observations.managementAction: They have started with the process of closing their remaining deposit with the Philippine National Bank.
observations.reason: Documentation process.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the above enumerated documents be submitted for auditorial review and to facilitate further the technical inspection/ evaluation by the COA Regional Technical Services of the said project.
